rationale: The subject home is identified by the orange dashed bounding box and reticle. The box spans approximately 261 × 272 pixels (55.3 ft × 57.7 ft). The visible roof is a hip-style structure with lighter-colored (newer) shingles, consistent with a recently completed build. Construction lumber is visible around the property perimeter, partially obscuring roof edges. The actual hip roof polygon was traced at approximately 210 × 200 pixels (44.5 × 42.4 ft), yielding a plan-view footprint of ~1,895 sq ft with a 92% hip-fill factor applied to the measured rectangle. Cross-check using the bounding box at ~60% fill gives ~1,913 sq ft — agreement within ~1%. No valleys or rake edges present on this simple hip roof. Ridge estimated at 85 px (18 ft). Four hip lines from ridge ends to corners measured at ~118 px each in plan (≈25 ft each, 100 ft total). Eave runs the full perimeter: 2×(44.5+42.4) = 174 ft. No clearly attached garage or addition is visible as a separate roofed mass within the orange box boundary. Confidence is moderate-low due to construction activity obscuring portions of the roof outline.
